title: How Ukrainian Fiction Teaches Courage
author: Marci Shore
subline: A reading of a people, in their own words.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- An open book: two facing pages with Ukrainian text lines. A red poppy flower (the Ukrainian national flower of remembrance) grows from the spine of the book. Title sits below. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1240)" fill="none" stroke-width="2">
    <!-- The book base: a wide open book -->
    <path d="M -700 0 L 0 60 L 700 0 L 700 360 L 0 420 L -700 360 Z" stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="2.5" fill="#0a0a0a"/>
    <!-- Spine line -->
    <line x1="0" y1="60" x2="0" y2="420" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
    <!-- Lines of text on the left page (faint, ash) -->
    <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="2" opacity="0.7">
      <line x1="-640" y1="100" x2="-60" y2="140"/>
      <line x1="-640" y1="140" x2="-60" y2="180"/>
      <line x1="-640" y1="180" x2="-60" y2="220"/>
      <line x1="-640" y1="220" x2="-200" y2="240"/>
      <line x1="-640" y1="260" x2="-60" y2="300"/>
      <line x1="-640" y1="300" x2="-60" y2="340"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Lines of text on the right page -->
    <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="2" opacity="0.7">
      <line x1="60" y1="140" x2="640" y2="100"/>
      <line x1="60" y1="180" x2="640" y2="140"/>
      <line x1="60" y1="220" x2="640" y2="180"/>
      <line x1="60" y1="260" x2="640" y2="220"/>
      <line x1="200" y1="240" x2="640" y2="260"/>
      <line x1="60" y1="300" x2="640" y2="300"/>
      <line x1="60" y1="340" x2="500" y2="360"/>
    </g>
    <!-- A single red highlighted line on the left page (a powerful sentence) -->
    <line x1="-640" y1="180" x2="-60" y2="220" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="3"/>
    <!-- A red poppy stem growing from the spine of the book, going UP -->
    <line x1="0" y1="60" x2="0" y2="-260" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2.5"/>
    <!-- Two small leaves -->
    <path d="M 0 -100 Q -40 -130 -60 -90" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2"/>
    <path d="M 0 -180 Q 40 -210 60 -170" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2"/>
    <!-- The poppy flower at the top — 4 red petals + center -->
    <circle cx="0" cy="-280" r="20" fill="#fc1c46"/>
    <circle cx="-22" cy="-300" r="14" fill="#fc1c46"/>
    <circle cx="22" cy="-300" r="14" fill="#fc1c46"/>
    <circle cx="0" cy="-318" r="14" fill="#fc1c46"/>
    <circle cx="0" cy="-280" r="6" fill="#0a0a0a"/>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: A literal open book with two facing pages, drawn in 3D perspective, with faint ash lines of Ukrainian text. ONE line on the left page is highlighted in RED 3px — a single powerful sentence from the literature. A red poppy stem grows UP from the spine of the book, ending in a 4-petal poppy at the top — the Ukrainian national symbol of remembrance. Title sits in the bottom 1/3, weight 300 whisper, mixed-case (3-line stack). Brand 'УКРАЇНА' in the top-left; pill 'KYIV' at the top.
